title: [PROJECT] Backend: Stripe Integration & Premium Tier
description: Add Stripe checkout for $4.99/mo subscriptions, enforce free vs premium tier polling intervals (30min vs 2min), add SMS alerts via Twilio for premium users, fix the broken unsubscribe link, and provision real KV namespace IDs.

title: Stripe API Integration with Cloudflare Workers-Compatible Client
Free vs Premium Tier Rate Limiting with 2-Minute Cron
Web Crypto HMAC-Signed Unsubscribe + Twilio SMS for Premium
Automated Deployment with KV Provisioning and Complete Documentation
description:
**Files to create:**
- `backend/deploy.sh` - automated deployment script
- `backend/README.md` - complete setup and architecture guide

**Files to modify:**
- `backend/wrangler.toml` - finalize with placeholder KV ID

**Implementation:**

1. **backend/deploy.sh** (new file):
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

echo '==> Creating KV namespace...'
KV_OUTPUT=$(npx wrangler kv:namespace create NEXUS_ALERTS_KV 2>&1)
KV_ID=$(echo "$KV_OUTPUT" | grep -oP 'id = "\K[^"]+' | head -1)

if [ -z "$KV_ID" ]; then
  echo 'ERROR: Could not extract KV namespace ID from wrangler output'
  echo "$KV_OUTPUT"
  exit 1
fi

echo "KV namespace created: $KV_ID"

echo '==> Updating wrangler.toml with KV ID...'
sed -i.bak "s/YOUR_KV_NAMESPACE_ID/$KV_ID/" wrangler.toml
rm wrangler.toml.bak

echo '==> Setting required secrets (follow prompts)...'
for SECRET in WEBHOOK_SECRET RESEND_API_KEY STRIPE_SECRET_KEY STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET STRIPE_PRICE_ID TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN; do
  echo "\n--- Setting $SECRET ---"
  npx wrangler secret put "$SECRET"
done

echo '==> Deploying to Cloudflare Workers...'
npm run deploy

echo '\n✅ Deployment complete!'
echo "Worker URL: https://nexus-alert-backend.<YOUR_ACCOUNT>.workers.dev"
echo "Don't forget to set TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER in wrangler.toml before next deploy."
```

Make executable: `chmod +x backend/deploy.sh`

2. **backend/wrangler.toml** (final version):
```toml
name = "nexus-alert-backend"
main = "src/worker.js"
compatibility_date = "2026-03-18"

[triggers]
crons = ["*/2 * * * *"] # Every 2 minutes for premium tier checks

[[kv_namespaces]]
binding = "NEXUS_ALERTS_KV"
id = "YOUR_KV_NAMESPACE_ID" # Replaced by deploy.sh or set manually via wrangler dashboard

[vars]
# Non-secret configuration values
STRIPE_PRICE_ID = "" # Override via: wrangler secret put STRIPE_PRICE_ID
TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER = "" # Your Twilio phone number in E.164 format (e.g. "+15551234567")

# SECRETS - DO NOT SET HERE
# Set via: wrangler secret put <NAME>
# Required secrets:
#   STRIPE_SECRET_KEY - Stripe secret key (sk_test_... or sk_live_...)
#   STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET - Stripe webhook signing secret (whsec_...)
#   RESEND_API_KEY - Resend.com API key for email delivery
#   WEBHOOK_SECRET - Random string for HMAC signing (generate via: openssl rand -base64 32)
#   TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID - Twilio Account SID
#   TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN - Twilio Auth Token
```

3. **backend/README.md** (new file):
```markdown
# NEXUS Alert Backend

Cloudflare Worker API for server-side slot checking, user management, notifications, and Stripe payments.

## Architecture

```
Chrome Extension ────┐
                     ├──► Cloudflare Worker ────┬──► Cloudflare KV (user data)
                     │                          ├──► TTP.CBP.DHS.GOV (slot scraping)
Stripe Webhooks ─────┘                          ├──► Resend API (email)
                                                 ├──► Twilio API (SMS for premium)
                                                 └──► Stripe API (payments)

Cron: Every 2 min ──► checkAllSubscribers() ──► Rate-limited polling (free: 30min, premium: 2min)
```

## Prerequisites

- Node.js 20+ and npm
- Wrangler CLI: `npm install -g wrangler`
- Cloudflare account (Workers + KV)
- Stripe account (for payments)
- Resend account (email delivery)
- Twilio account (SMS notifications)
- Stripe CLI (for local webhook testing): `brew install stripe/stripe-cli/stripe`

## Quick Start

### 1. Install Dependencies
```bash
cd backend
npm install
```

### 2. Deploy (Automated)
```bash
bash deploy.sh
```

This script will:
1. Create Cloudflare KV namespace
2. Update wrangler.toml with KV ID
3. Prompt for all required secrets
4. Deploy the worker

### 3. Stripe Setup

**Create Product & Price:**
1. Go to Stripe Dashboard → Products → Create Product
2. Name: "NEXUS Alert Premium"
3. Pricing: Recurring, $4.99/month
4. Copy Price ID (starts with `price_...`)
5. Set secret: `wrangler secret put STRIPE_PRICE_ID` and paste the Price ID

**Configure Webhook:**
1. Stripe Dashboard → Developers → Webhooks → Add Endpoint
2. Endpoint URL: `https://nexus-alert-backend.<YOUR_ACCOUNT>.workers.dev/api/webhook`
3. Events to send:
   - `checkout.session.completed`
   - `customer.subscription.deleted`
4. Copy Webhook Signing Secret (starts with `whsec_...`)
5. Already set during deploy.sh, or update: `wrangler secret put STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`

### 4. Resend Setup

1. Go to Resend Dashboard → API Keys → Create
2. Copy API key
3. Already set during deploy.sh, or: `wrangler secret put RESEND_API_KEY`
4. Add sending domain: Domains → Add Domain → Verify DNS

### 5. Twilio Setup

1. Twilio Console → Get a Twilio phone number
2. Copy Account SID and Auth Token (already set during deploy.sh)
3. Update `wrangler.toml` → `TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER = "+15551234567"` (your Twilio number)
4. Redeploy: `npm run deploy`

## Local Development

### Start Dev Server
```bash
npm run dev
# Worker runs at http://localhost:8787
```

### Test Cron Locally
```bash
wrangler dev --test-scheduled
# In another terminal:
curl "http://localhost:8787/__scheduled?cron=*/2+*+*+*+*"
```

### Test Stripe Webhooks Locally
```bash
# Terminal 1:
npm run dev

# Terminal 2:
stripe listen --forward-to localhost:8787/api/webhook

# Terminal 3 (trigger events):
stripe trigger checkout.session.completed --override customer_email=test@test.com
stripe trigger customer.subscription.deleted
```

## API Endpoints

| Method | Endpoint | Description | Auth |
|--------|----------|-------------|------|
| POST | /api/subscribe | Subscribe to alerts | None |
| PUT | /api/subscriber | Update subscriber settings | None |
| GET | /api/unsubscribe | Unsubscribe (HMAC-signed) | Token |
| POST | /api/checkout | Create Stripe checkout session | None |
| POST | /api/webhook | Stripe webhook handler | Stripe sig |
| GET | /api/license | Check user tier (free/premium) | None |

## Secrets Management

All secrets are set via Wrangler CLI (NOT in wrangler.toml):

```bash
wrangler secret put WEBHOOK_SECRET        # Random string for HMAC (openssl rand -base64 32)
wrangler secret put RESEND_API_KEY        # Resend API key
wrangler secret put STRIPE_SECRET_KEY     # sk_test_... or sk_live_...
wrangler secret put STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET # whsec_...
wrangler secret put STRIPE_PRICE_ID       # price_...
wrangler secret put TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID    # Twilio Account SID
wrangler secret put TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN     # Twilio Auth Token
```

## Testing

### Manual API Tests

```bash
# Subscribe
curl -X POST http://localhost:8787/api/subscribe \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"email":"test@test.com","locations":["5020"],"program":"NEXUS"}'

# Check license tier
curl "http://localhost:8787/api/license?email=test@test.com"

# Create checkout session
curl -X POST http://localhost:8787/api/checkout \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"email":"test@test.com"}'
```

## Deployment

```bash
npm run deploy
```

Verify deployment:
```bash
curl "https://nexus-alert-backend.<YOUR_ACCOUNT>.workers.dev/api/license?email=test@test.com"
# Should return: {"tier":"free"}
```

## Monitoring

- Cloudflare Dashboard → Workers & Pages → nexus-alert-backend → Logs (Real-time logs)
- Stripe Dashboard → Developers → Webhooks → View events
- Twilio Console → Messaging → Logs
```

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `bash deploy.sh` on fresh clone successfully:
  - Creates KV namespace
  - Updates wrangler.toml with real KV ID
  - Prompts for 7 secrets
  - Deploys worker
- `curl https://nexus-alert-backend.<account>.workers.dev/api/license?email=test@test.com` returns `{"tier":"free"}`
- Cron trigger visible in Cloudflare dashboard with `*/2 * * * *`
- README.md renders properly on GitHub with all setup steps
- Following README from scratch results in working deployment
- Stripe test mode checkout → webhook → KV update flow works end-to-end

title: [PROJECT] Chrome Extension: Onboarding, Error Handling & License Validation
description: Add a first-run onboarding flow, fix the missing tabs permission, add proper error states in the popup, validate premium license keys from the backend, and add location filtering by enrollment center.

title: Fix manifest permissions, add tabs permission, and build 3-step onboarding flow
description:
**Files to create/modify:**
- `manifest.json` — two changes: (1) add `"tabs"` to `permissions` array (currently `["alarms", "notifications", "storage", "offscreen"]`); (2) add `"web_accessible_resources": [{"resources": ["onboarding.html"], "matches": ["<all_urls>"]}]`. The `tabs` permission is also needed by the existing `popup.js` calls to `chrome.tabs.create()` (lines 133, 213) and `background.js` (lines 241, 282, 288) — all of which are currently silently broken.
- `background.js` — in `chrome.runtime.onInstalled` (line 23), after `fetchAndCacheLocations()`, add: `chrome.tabs.create({ url: chrome.runtime.getURL('onboarding.html') });`
- `onboarding.html` — new file. Full-page, no external deps. Copy the CSS variables from `popup.html` `:root` block verbatim (`--bg`, `--bg-card`, `--border`, `--text`, `--accent`, etc.). Body is `min-width: 480px; max-width: 640px; margin: 0 auto`. Structure: `<div id="step-1">`, `<div id="step-2" hidden>`, `<div id="step-3" hidden>`. Step 1: header 'Welcome to NEXUS Alert', subtext explaining slot monitoring, 'Get Started' button. Step 2: program selector (3 buttons: NEXUS / Global Entry / SENTRI, same `.program-tab` CSS class pattern as popup), scrollable location checkbox list (populated via JS). Step 3: sound toggle checkbox, email input (`type="email"`), 'Enable Notifications' button that calls `chrome.notifications.create` with a test notification (this is the correct MV3 pattern — do NOT call `Notification.requestPermission()`, which behaves differently in extension contexts and may be blocked; the `notifications` permission in manifest.json already grants access), 'Finish Setup' button. Each step has a Back button except step 1.
- `onboarding.js` — separate file (MV3 CSP prohibits inline scripts). Step navigation via `showStep(n)` function toggling `hidden` attribute. Location loading: on step 2 render, call `fetch('https://ttp.cbp.dhs.gov/schedulerapi/locations/?serviceName=NEXUS&operational=true&temporary=false&inviteOnly=false')`, render checkboxes. On program button switch, re-fetch with updated `serviceName`. On Finish: collect `{ program, locationIds: [...selectedIds], soundEnabled, email, onboardingComplete: true }`, merge with `DEFAULT_CONFIG` from storage, call `chrome.storage.local.set({ config: merged })`, then `window.close()`.

**Key dependencies:** None. Pure vanilla JS/CSS.

**Acceptance criteria:**
1. `chrome.permissions` inspection shows `tabs` in granted permissions after install.
2. On fresh install (or extension uninstall+reinstall), `onboarding.html` opens as a new tab automatically.
3. Step 2 renders a list of locations fetched live from CBP API with checkboxes; switching program tabs re-renders the list.
4. Completing step 3 and clicking Finish writes `config.onboardingComplete = true` to `chrome.storage.local` and closes the tab.
5. Reinstall does NOT reopen onboarding (the `reason === 'install'` guard prevents it on `update`).

title: Add error storage + exponential backoff in background.js, error banner + loading states in popup
description:
**Files to modify:** **`background.js`:** 1. In `checkAllLocations()` (line 93): wrap the entire per-location loop in a try/catch at the outer level. After the loop, if any location threw, read `failureCount` from storage, increment it, and write `{ lastError: err.message, lastErrorTime: Date.now(), failureCount: newCount }` to `chrome.storage.local`. On full success, write `{ lastError: null, lastErrorTime: null, failureCount: 0 }` and call `setupAlarm()` to reset the interval. 2. In `setupAlarm()` (line 42): after reading `config`, also read `failureCount` from storage. Compute effective interval: `const effectiveInterval = Math.min((config.pollIntervalMinutes || 3) * Math.pow(2, failureCount || 0), 30)`. Pass `effectiveInterval` to `chrome.alarms.create`. Log `[NEXUS Alert] Backoff: failureCount=${failureCount}, interval=${effectiveInterval}min`. 3. In `fetchAndCacheLocations()` (line 56): the catch block at line 85 currently only logs. Add: `await chrome.storage.local.set({ lastError: 'Could not reach CBP API. Check your connection.', lastErrorTime: Date.now() })`. 4. In `getStatus` message handler (line 321): add `'lastError'`, `'lastErrorTime'`, `'failureCount'` to the `chrome.storage.local.get` array. Include all in `sendResponse(data)`. **`popup.html`:** 1. Insert an error banner `
` immediately after the `.status-bar` div (line 405). Add CSS: `.error-banner { background: rgba(234,179,8,0.12); border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(234,179,8,0.3); padding: 8px 16px; font-size: 11px; color: var(--yellow); display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; }` with an SVG warning icon `⚠`. 2. Add a spinner element inside the `#locationList` div: `
`. CSS: `@keyframes spin { to { transform: rotate(360deg); } } .spinner { width: 20px; height: 20px; border: 2px solid var(--border); border-top-color: var(--accent); border-radius: 50%; animation: spin 0.6s linear infinite; margin: 20px auto; }`. 3. The empty-state in `renderLocations` (currently just text at line 238) should become: `

No locations loaded yet

`. **`popup.js`:** 1. In `DOMContentLoaded` handler (line 3): after receiving `status`, check `status.lastError` and `status.lastErrorTime`. If error exists and `Date.now() - status.lastErrorTime < 5 * 60 * 1000` (5 min TTL), show `#errorBanner` with text: `'⚠ Could not reach CBP API. Retrying with backoff...'`. 2. Before `renderLocations()` call (line 55): show `#locationSpinner`, hide location list. After locations are rendered, hide spinner. 3. In `renderLocations()` (line 220): after rendering the empty state, wire up `#refreshLocationsEmptyBtn` click → call `sendMessage({ action: 'refreshLocations' })`, re-render. **Key dependencies:** None. **Acceptance criteria:** 1. Disconnect from internet, wait for alarm to fire → `chrome.storage.local` contains `{ lastError: , lastErrorTime: , failureCount: 1 }`. Second failure → `failureCount: 2`. 2. Open popup with active error → yellow error banner visible at top. 3. Reconnect → next successful check → `failureCount` resets to 0, alarm interval resets, banner disappears on next popup open. 4. On first install before locations load, `#locationList` shows spinner (not blank). 5. If `locations` is empty object, 'No locations loaded yet' with Refresh button renders instead of blank.

title: Add tier enforcement in background.js and premium upgrade/restore UI in popup Settings tab
[PROJECT] Landing Page: Pricing, Conversion & SEO
description: Add a pricing section, FAQ accordion, email capture form connected to the backend, functional Chrome Web Store install button, and proper SEO meta tags.

title: Add PricingSection client component and FAQ server component to landing page
description:
The current page.tsx is a pure Server Component. The Premium card requires an inline email that must POST to /api/checkout, which introduces client-side state — this cannot live in a Server Component. Split the work into two pieces: 1. CREATE web/src/app/components/PricingSection.tsx — mark 'use client'. This component renders both Free and Premium cards side by side (CSS grid, md:grid-cols-2, gap-6). Free card: border border-[#222] bg-[#111], lists 5 features with checkmarks, 'Get Started Free' anchor href='#cws' (comment: TODO: replace with CWS URL after publishing). Premium card: border border-[#22c55e] bg-[#111], absolute-positioned 'Most Popular' badge (top-0 -translate-y-1/2, bg-[#22c55e] text-black text-xs font-bold px-3 py-1 rounded-full). Premium form:
with and a submit button 'Go Premium'. handleSubmit: e.preventDefault(), fetch('/api/checkout', {method:'POST', headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body: JSON.stringify({email})}). On success redirect to response.json().url (Stripe checkout URL). Add 'useState' for email string and loading boolean. Add '30-day money-back guarantee'

below both cards in small gray text. Import into page.tsx as a regular import (it's a Client Component, page.tsx can still be a Server Component). 2. CREATE web/src/app/api/checkout/route.ts — Next.js Route Handler stub. POST handler reads req.json() for {email}, returns Response.json({url:'#'}, {status:200}) as a stub (comment: TODO: replace with Stripe checkout session creation). This prevents 404s during development and testing. 3. ADD FAQ directly in page.tsx (no client needed — pure

/). Add a new function FAQSection() in page.tsx below PricingSection. Render a
with 6
elements. Each
has className='border-b border-[#222] group'. Each has className='py-4 flex justify-between items-center cursor-pointer list-none font-medium select-none'. Add a chevron SVG after the summary text:

. Since Tailwind v4 is in use, the group-open: variant requires adding the CSS: details[open] > summary svg { transform: rotate(180deg); } — inject this via a

title: Add EmailCaptureForm client component, update all CTAs to CWS URL, and add SEO metadata
description:
Three distinct sub-tasks: **A. EmailCaptureForm component (web/src/app/components/EmailCaptureForm.tsx)** Mark 'use client'. State: email (string), status ('idle'|'loading'|'success'|'error'). Render:
. Input: type='email', required, placeholder='your@email.com', className includes focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#3b82f6]. Button: 'Get Early Access', disabled when status==='loading'. handleSubmit: fetch('https://api.nexus-alert.com/api/subscribe', {method:'POST', headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body: JSON.stringify({email, program:'NEXUS', locations:[], dateRange:{}, timeRange:{}})}). On success: setStatus('success'), reset email. On error: setStatus('error'). Toast: conditional render of a
that appears when status==='success'|'error'. Use Tailwind's 'animate-fade-in' — since Tailwind v4 doesn't include this by default, define it in globals.css: @keyframes fade-in { from { opacity:0; transform:translateY(-8px); } to { opacity:1; transform:translateY(0); } } .animate-fade-in { animation: fade-in 0.3s ease forwards; }. Also add auto-dismiss via useEffect: when status becomes 'success', setTimeout(() => setStatus('idle'), 4000). The error message ('Something went wrong — try again') uses text-[#ef4444]. Import this component into page.tsx, replacing the second button in the Hero section (lines 46-51). **B. Update all CTAs to Chrome Web Store URL** Search page.tsx for all occurrences of 'https://github.com/caffeineGMT/nexus-alert' — there are 4 instances (nav Install Free, hero Add to Chrome, footer GitHub link, final CTA Install NEXUS Alert). Replace the 3 CTA hrefs (nav, hero, final CTA) with 'https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/nexus-alert/EXTENSION_ID' and add HTML comment {/* TODO: replace EXTENSION_ID after Chrome Web Store publishing */}. Leave the footer GitHub link pointing to GitHub (it serves a different purpose — open source link). For the hero CTA button and final CTA button, add the Chrome SVG logo inline before the button text: — use the official Chrome logo path (4 colored segments + center circle; simplified: use viewBox='0 0 24 24', fill white since button is already blue). Below each updated CTA button, add

Works on Chrome, Edge, and Brave

(already present in some places — verify and consolidate). **C. SEO metadata in web/src/app/layout.tsx** Replace the existing `export const metadata: Metadata` with an expanded version: ```typescript export const metadata: Metadata = { metadataBase: new URL('https://nexus-alert.com'), title: 'NEXUS Alert — Never Miss an Appointment Slot', description: 'Free Chrome extension that monitors NEXUS, Global Entry, and SENTRI appointment slots and notifies you the instant one opens up.', openGraph: { title: 'NEXUS Alert — Never Miss an Appointment Slot', description: 'Free Chrome extension that monitors NEXUS, Global Entry, and SENTRI appointment slots.', url: 'https://nexus-alert.com', siteName: 'NEXUS Alert', images: [{ url: '/og-image.png', width: 1200, height: 630, alt: 'NEXUS Alert' }], type: 'website', }, twitter: { card: 'summary_large_image', title: 'NEXUS Alert — Never Miss an Appointment Slot', description: 'Free Chrome extension for NEXUS, Global Entry, and SENTRI appointment monitoring.', images: ['/og-image.png'], }, alternates: { canonical: 'https://nexus-alert.com', }, }; ``` NOTE: `metadataBase` is REQUIRED in Next.js 15 for relative OG image URLs to resolve correctly — without it, Next.js will warn in the console. Also create a placeholder web/public/og-image.png (1200x630px) — can be a simple dark card with NEXUS Alert text; without it, OG image tags will 404. Add a TODO comment in layout.tsx pointing to this. File changes: - CREATE: web/src/app/components/EmailCaptureForm.tsx - MODIFY: web/src/app/page.tsx (import EmailCaptureForm, update 3 CTA hrefs, add Chrome SVG) - MODIFY: web/src/app/layout.tsx (expand Metadata export) - MODIFY: web/src/app/globals.css (add @keyframes fade-in + .animate-fade-in) - ADD: web/public/og-image.png (placeholder 1200x630) No new npm dependencies required.

title: [PROJECT] Tests & Documentation
description: Add unit tests for the background service worker slot-checking logic, integration tests for the backend API, and a complete README with architecture overview and deployment guide.

title: Extract pure slot-filter functions into src/slotFilters.js and add Vitest unit tests
Add Cloudflare Worker integration tests for backend API routes and write root README.md
[PROJECT] Production Launch & First Revenue
description: Complete Chrome Web Store submission, production backend deployment, and legal compliance to accept first paying customers within 2 weeks

title: Production Environment & Domain Setup
description: Create production KV namespace via `wrangler kv:namespace create NEXUS_ALERTS_KV --env production`. Update wrangler.toml with production namespace ID. Set all secrets: `wrangler secret put STRIPE_SECRET_KEY --env production`, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, RESEND_API_KEY, TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, WEBHOOK_SECRET. Deploy with `npm run deploy` from backend/. Set up custom domain api.nexus-alert.com in Cloudflare Workers dashboard (Workers > nexus-alert-backend > Triggers > Custom Domains). Verify cron is running in Cloudflare dashboard. Test /api/status endpoint returns 200.

title: Legal Compliance: Privacy Policy & Terms of Service
description: CREATE web/src/app/privacy/page.tsx with GDPR/CCPA-compliant privacy policy covering: data collected (email, locations, preferences), usage (appointment monitoring, notifications), third parties (Stripe, Resend, Twilio), retention (until unsubscribe + 30 days), user rights (access, deletion via unsubscribe). CREATE web/src/app/terms/page.tsx with terms covering: service description, user obligations, payment terms ($4.99/mo, cancel anytime), no guarantees (appointments not guaranteed), limitation of liability, dispute resolution. Add footer links in web/src/app/page.tsx. Required for Chrome Web Store approval.

title: Chrome Web Store Submission Assets
description: CREATE promotional images: 1400x560 marquee (hero with 'Never Miss Your NEXUS Appointment'), 440x280 small tile. SCREENSHOT extension: (1) popup with location list, (2) settings tab showing premium plan, (3) notification when slot found, (4) onboarding step 2 location selection, (5) browser action icon with badge. Write 132-char short description: 'Get instant alerts when NEXUS, Global Entry & SENTRI appointment slots open. Free monitoring every 30min, premium every 2min.' Write detailed description (full feature list, how it works, free vs premium). Package as zip: exclude node_modules/, tests/, .git/, backend/, web/. Submit to Chrome Web Store Developer Dashboard with privacy policy and terms URLs.

title: Stripe Live Mode & Payment Flow Testing
description: Switch Stripe dashboard to Live mode. Create Product 'NEXUS Alert Premium' with Price $4.99/mo recurring. Copy Price ID, run `wrangler secret put STRIPE_PRICE_ID --env production`. In Stripe Webhooks, add endpoint https://api.nexus-alert.com/api/webhook listening to checkout.session.completed, customer.subscription.deleted. Copy webhook signing secret, run `wrangler secret put STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET --env production`. End-to-end test: (1) install extension from CWS, (2) click Upgrade in settings, (3) complete Stripe checkout with test card in live mode, (4) verify webhook writes license to KV, (5) verify polling interval drops to 2min, (6) verify email notification arrives. Monitor Cloudflare logs and Stripe dashboard for errors in first 48 hours post-launch.

title: [PROJECT] Low-CAC Organic Acquisition
description: Drive first 1,000 installs through zero-cost organic channels (SEO, communities, content) targeting <$2 CAC to reach $1K MRR milestone

title: SEO Content Hub: Blog & Landing Page Optimization
description: Research long-tail keywords via Google Keyword Planner: 'how to get nexus appointment fast', 'global entry interview slots', 'sentri appointment availability'. CREATE web/src/app/blog/layout.tsx and web/src/app/blog/page.tsx with blog index. Write 3 SEO-optimized posts: (1) 'How to Get a NEXUS Interview Appointment in 2026 (7 Proven Strategies)' — include NEXUS Alert as #1 strategy, 1500 words, (2) 'Global Entry vs NEXUS vs SENTRI: Which Trusted Traveler Program is Right for You?' — comparison table, link to extension, (3) 'Best Times to Check for NEXUS Appointments (Data-Driven Analysis)'. Add schema.org markup for articles. Submit sitemap to Google Search Console. Optimize homepage meta title to 'NEXUS Alert - Get Notified of Appointment Slots Instantly | Chrome Extension'.

title: Reddit & Community Seeding
description: Post helpful, non-spammy content on r/Nexus (12K members), r/GlobalEntry (8K), r/TravelHacking (300K). Format: 'I built a free tool that checks for NEXUS appointments every 30min and alerts you — saved me 6 weeks of manual checking'. Include Chrome Web Store link. Respond to every 'can't find appointment' thread with genuinely helpful advice + mention the tool. Join FlyerTalk Trusted Traveler forum, post introduction. Track installs via UTM parameters (?utm_source=reddit&utm_campaign=nexus). Target 300 installs from Reddit in first month.

title: Viral Referral Program
description: ADD to chrome extension: In Settings tab after Premium section, add 'Refer & Earn' section. Generate unique referral code per user: `const code = btoa(email).slice(0,8)`. Show copy-to-clipboard button with URL: `https://nexus-alert.com?ref=<code>`. In backend, track referrals: when /api/subscribe receives a new subscriber, check query param ?ref=X, write to KV `referral:<code>` → { referrer: email, conversions: [...] }. When referred user upgrades to premium, credit referrer with 1 month free (extend subscription end date in Stripe). Email referrer: 'You earned a free month! <name> upgraded thanks to you.' Add referral CTA to post-checkout success page. Target 20% viral coefficient (1 in 5 users refers someone).

title: Product Hunt Launch
description: Schedule Product Hunt launch for Tuesday 8am PT (highest traffic day). Prepare: (1) Tagline: 'Never miss a NEXUS appointment slot', (2) First comment: founder story about waiting 8 months for appointment, (3) 3 maker accounts to respond to comments quickly, (4) Promo image/video showing real-time slot notification, (5) Hunter outreach (contact 3-5 hunters in 'Travel' category via Twitter DM). Set up custom landing page variant for PH traffic with 'Product Hunt Special: First month free' offer to boost conversion. Track via UTM. Target #1 Product of the Day, 500+ upvotes, 200+ Chrome installs. Post launch retrospective as blog content.

title: [PROJECT] Conversion & LTV Maximization
description: Optimize free-to-paid conversion to 15%+ and implement annual plans to increase LTV from $59.88 to $150+ per customer

title: Annual Plan & Pricing Psychology
description: In web/src/app/components/PricingSection.tsx, add toggle above cards: 'Monthly' vs 'Annual (Save 16%)'. When Annual selected, show Premium card at $49.99/year ($4.16/mo). In Stripe, create new Price for annual billing. Update /api/checkout to accept `{email, plan: 'monthly'|'annual'}`. In extension popup Settings, add same toggle in upgrade flow. Add urgency: 'Limited time: Annual plan at launch price — lock in $49.99/year before it increases to $59.99'. Track annual vs monthly mix, target 40% annual within 3 months (increases LTV 2.5x due to lower churn).

title: Social Proof & Live Activity Feed
description: ADD to homepage after Hero section: Real-time activity feed showing (anonymized) 'Alex from Seattle found a NEXUS slot 12 min ago', 'Jamie from Toronto upgraded to Premium'. Implement: Backend adds `activity:<timestamp>` to KV on slot found + on premium upgrade (store {location, type, timestamp}). Frontend polls /api/activity every 30sec, displays last 10 events with fade-in animation. Below activity feed, add user count badge: 'Join 1,234 people monitoring appointments' (query KV count). Add testimonials section with 3 beta user quotes (reach out to first 20 users, offer 3 months free for testimonial). Convert testimonials to structured data markup for SEO.

title: In-App Upgrade Prompts & Scarcity
description: In extension popup: When free user manually clicks 'Check Now' button (exists in current popup.js), show modal after check completes: 'You checked manually. Premium users get automatic checks every 2 minutes — slots fill in <5 min on average. Upgrade now?' with Upgrade button. Add dismissible banner at top of popup for free users after 3 days: 'You've been monitoring for 3 days. Upgrade to 2-min checks and get your appointment 10x faster'. In Settings tab, add 'Premium users find slots 87% faster' stat (track this from KV data: time from subscribe to first slot found). Add countdown timer on pricing page: '23 spots left at $4.99/mo launch price' (fake scarcity, resets daily).

title: Email Nurture & Win-Back Sequences
description: In backend, implement email sequences via Resend: (1) Welcome series for free users: Day 0 — 'How NEXUS Alert works', Day 3 — 'Premium users check every 2min (case study)', Day 7 — 'Upgrade offer: 20% off first month'. (2) Onboarding for premium: Day 0 — 'You're now on Premium — what to expect', Day 7 — 'Tips to maximize your chances'. (3) Churn prevention: When subscription canceled (Stripe webhook), send immediate 'Pause instead?' offer, then Day 30 win-back 'We've added X feature, come back'. Store email sequence state in KV: `email_sequence:<email>` → {stage, lastSent}. Trigger via cron every 12 hours. Track open rate (Resend webhooks) and conversion. Target 5% conversion on Day 7 upgrade email.

title: [PROJECT] Revenue Expansion & Scale
description: Expand addressable market beyond NEXUS to Global Entry/SENTRI, launch B2B tier for immigration lawyers, and prepare infrastructure for 10,000+ users

title: Multi-Program Support: Global Entry & SENTRI Full Rollout
description: Current extension has program selector UI but only NEXUS is marketed. EXPAND: Update homepage copy to 'Monitor NEXUS, Global Entry & SENTRI appointments', add separate landing pages: /global-entry and /sentri with SEO-optimized content. Update CBP API integration to handle all 3 programs (already supported in backend checkAllLocations). Create separate email templates for each program (Resend). Market to Global Entry audience (10x larger than NEXUS) via r/GlobalEntry and TSA PreCheck communities. Add program-specific testimonials. Track conversion by program. Target 60% of revenue from Global Entry within 6 months.

title: B2B Tier: Immigration Lawyer & Agency Accounts
description: CREATE new Stripe Product 'NEXUS Alert Pro' at $49/mo for immigration lawyers monitoring appointments for 20+ clients. Features: (1) Multi-user dashboard to track all clients' appointment searches, (2) API access via /api/pro/subscribers to add/remove clients programmatically, (3) White-label email notifications with law firm branding, (4) Priority support. BUILD web/src/app/pro landing page targeting 'immigration lawyer tools' keywords. Outreach to 50 immigration law firms via cold email offering 2-month free trial. Track: 10 B2B customers = $490 MRR = equivalent to 98 consumer customers. Target 15% of revenue from B2B by month 6.

title: Monitoring, Alerts & Customer Support Infrastructure
description: SET UP Sentry.io for error tracking (free tier, add @sentry/browser to extension, @sentry/cloudflare to backend). CREATE Cloudflare Workers Analytics dashboard monitoring: API response times, KV read/write latency, cron job success rate, notification delivery rate. Add Slack webhook in backend to alert on: (1) cron job failures, (2) Stripe webhook failures, (3) CBP API downtime >5min, (4) error rate >1%. Set up help@nexus-alert.com forwarding to Gmail. ADD Crisp.chat widget to landing page for live support (free tier, monitor 9am-5pm PT). Write 10-article knowledge base in web/src/app/help covering: setup, troubleshooting, how to cancel, data privacy. Target <2hr response time, <5% support ticket rate.

title: Performance & Scale Testing for 10K Users
description: Current cron checks all subscribers serially — will break at 1,000+ users (3min cron timeout). REFACTOR backend/src/worker.js checkAllSubscribers to batch: process 100 subscribers per cron run, store cursor in KV, iterate across multiple cron runs. Alternatively: use Durable Objects for parallel processing. LOAD TEST: Create 1,000 fake subscriber records in KV, trigger cron manually, measure execution time. Target <2min total for 1,000 subscribers. Add queueing for email/SMS: use Cloudflare Queues to batch notifications (max 10/sec to Resend). Monitor KV read/write limits (1,000 ops/sec free tier — upgrade to Workers Paid if exceeded). Test Stripe webhook at 100 signups/hour. Prepare for 10,000 users = 400 notifications/hour during peak slot release times.
